---
title: "Enhanced_FetchGabraA2_HG38"
format: html
editor: visual
---
# Getting Enhanced GABRA2 Information

This is some code loosely based on this \[Gemini Query\](<https://share.gemini.google/YDIW2mLOKgYd>), wherein I explore the use of an annotated SequenceRecord in Biopython. This extends what I played around with a bit in FetchGabraA2_HG38.py.

Next steps could include:

- Grabbing the record for each protein

- For each protein (enumerate the relevant articles from pubmed), printing title, author, and

```{python}
from Bio import Entrez, SeqIO

Entrez.email = "johnlockwood2999@gmail.com"

# Fetch GenBank format instead of FASTA
handle = Entrez.efetch(db="nucleotide", 
                       id="NC_000004.12", 
                       rettype="gbwithparts", # Crucial change here
                       retmode="text", 
                       seq_start=46243548, 
                       seq_stop=46390300)

# Read the record using the "genbank" parser
record = SeqIO.read(handle, "genbank")
print(record)

# import pdb; pdb.set_trace()
```

```{python}
# Filter for Coding Sequences (CDS)
cds_features = [f for f in record.features if f.type == "CDS"]

if not cds_features:
    print("No coding sequences found in this region.")
else:
    print("There are {0} coding sequences (splice variants) found".format(len(cds_features)))
    # A region might have multiple splice variants. We will grab the first one.
    main_cds = cds_features[0]
    
    # .extract() automatically stitches the exons together into the final mRNA format
    coding_sequence = main_cds.extract(record.seq)
    
    # We can pull the protein name from the GenBank qualifiers dictionary
    protein_name = main_cds.qualifiers.get('product', ['Unknown'])[0]
    
    print(f"Found product: {protein_name}")
    print(f"Stitched CDS length: {len(coding_sequence)} base pairs")
```

```{python}
# Filter for Coding Sequences
cds_features = [f for f in record.features if f.type == "CDS"]

print(f"Total splice variants found: {len(cds_features)}\n")

protein_ids = []

# Get basic info for each sequence variant
for index, cds in enumerate(cds_features):
    # print("CDS ------------------------------")
    # print(cds)
    # print("END CDS --------------------------")
    
    # Safely extract metadata from the qualifiers dictionary
    product = cds.qualifiers.get('product', ['Unknown Product'])[0]
    protein_id = cds.qualifiers.get('protein_id', ['No ID'])[0]
    
    # Save off for later processing.
    protein_ids.append(protein_id)
    
    # Extract and stitch the specific exons for this variant
    variant_seq = cds.extract(record.seq)
    
    # Translate to amino acids
    protein_seq = variant_seq.translate()
    
    # Print the profile of the variant
    print(f"--- Variant {index + 1} ---")
    print(f"Product: {product}")
    print(f"Protein ID: {protein_id}")
    print(f"CDS Length: {len(variant_seq)} bp")
    print(f"Protein Length: {len(protein_seq)} amino acids")
    print(f"First 10 Amino Acids: {protein_seq[:10]}\n")
    
print("-------------------------")
print("Protein IDs:", protein_ids)
```

Go after the first protein record for now.

```{python}
# Fetch the 1st protein record for now
handle = Entrez.efetch(db="protein", 
                       id=protein_ids[0], # Insert your protein_id here
                       rettype="gb", 
                       retmode="text")

protein_record = SeqIO.read(handle, "genbank")

# print(protein_record)

# The features list now contains protein-specific metadata
for feature in protein_record.features:
    if feature.type == "Region":
        domain_name = feature.qualifiers.get('region_name', [''])[0]
        print(f"Functional Domain: {domain_name} at {feature.location}")
```

More to do here. Here we just grab the number of articles available. There are suspiciously 10 many times – likely many repeats. Can use

```{python}

unique_pubmed_ids = set()

# Extract the linked PubMed IDs (PMIDs)
for protein_id in protein_ids:
  link_handle = Entrez.elink(dbfrom="protein", db="pubmed", id=protein_id)
  link_record = Entrez.read(link_handle)
  
  pubmed_ids = []
  for linkset in link_record[0]['LinkSetDb']:
    if linkset['LinkName'] == 'protein_pubmed':
        pubmed_ids = [link['Id'] for link in linkset['Link']]
        unique_pubmed_ids.update(pubmed_ids)
        
  print("There are {0} pubmed articles for protein {1}".format(len(pubmed_ids), protein_id))

print("There are {0} unique pubmed ids".format(len(unique_pubmed_ids)))
  
  
```
